merger_name: AUB – further interest in KJ Risk

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
Austbrokers Corporate Pty Ltd (trading as **AUC**), a directly and wholly-owned subsidiary of AUB Group Limited (**AUB**), proposes to acquire an additional 43% of the issued share capital in KJ Risk Group Pty Ltd (**KJ Risk**) by way of a purchase of shares from the existing majority shareholder, Niven Industries Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Niven Industries Trust (**Niven Industries**) (the **Acquisition**).

As part of the transaction, AUB Group Limited will transfer its existing 49% interest in KJ Risk to AUC. On completion, AUC will hold 92% of the issued share capital in KJ Risk, with the balance retained by Niven Industries.

AUC is a wholly owned subsidiary of AUB Group Limited (ASX: AUB), which itself is an ASX200 global insurance broking group with a diversified portfolio spanning General and Specialist Retail Brokers, Authorised Representative and Broking Networks, Managing General Agents and Underwriting Agencies, Wholesale Brokers, InsurTech platforms, and specialist Claims and Loss Adjustment services. Operating from approximately 580 locations, ~6,000 people support around 1.2 million clients and place more than AUD 11 billion in premiums with local and international insurers each year.

KJ Risk is an Australian risk management intermediary business. KJ Risk’s primary activities relate to discretionary mutual fund (**DMF**) services, including DMF broking and management services. KJ Risk also supplies general insurance broking services to a limited extent, including corporate and private client broking and workers compensation consulting services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Austbrokers Corporate Pty Ltd (trading as AUC), a directly and
wholly-owned subsidiary of AUB Group Limited (AUB), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 43% of
the issued share capital in KJ Risk Group Pty Ltd (KJ Risk), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
As part of the Acquisition, AUB will transfer its existing 49% interest
in KJ Risk to AUC.
On completion, AUC will hold 92% of the issued share capital in KJ
Risk, with the balance retained by Niven Industries Pty Ltd, as trustee
for the Niven Industries Trust, which is the existing majority
shareholder.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AUC, is a wholly owned subsidiary of AUB. AUB is an
ASX200 global insurance broking group with a portfolio that includes
General and Specialist Retail Brokers, Authorised Representative and
Broking Networks, Managing General Agents and Underwriting
Agencies, Wholesale Brokers, InsurTech platforms, and specialist
Claims and Loss Adjustment services.
The target, KJ Risk, is an Australian risk management intermediary
business. KJ Risk’s primary activities relate to discretionary mutual
fund (DMF) services, including DMF broking and management
services. KJ Risk also supplies general insurance broking services,
including corporate and private client broking and workers
compensation consulting services.
KJ Risk operates as an integrated member of AUB, conducting its
business exclusively within the AUB network and under the AUB
brand, governance and operational framework.
AUB and KJ Risk overlap in the supply of general insurance broking
services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
• Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between AUB (and its
connected entities) and KJ Risk in the supply of general
insurance broking services in Australia
b. there are alternative suppliers of general insurance broking
services to customers in Australia
c. except for AUB’s existing interest in KJ Risk, AUB (and its
connected entities) does not supply DMF broking or
management services in Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
